100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 22, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, where discussions centered on disarmament and post-war recovery efforts. In the United States, the Senate debated President Coolidge's proposed tax cuts amid growing concerns over the economy. Meanwhile, the British Empire faced tensions in India as protests against colonial rule intensified. In the realm of culture, the latest edition of the popular comic strip "Little Orphan Annie" captivated readers across the nation.
